flagship.title: Melnikov threshold vs period-doubling onset: a quantitative gap map
flagship.thesis: For the damped driven pendulum at omega=2/3, the analytic Melnikov homoclinic-tangle threshold and the measured period-doubling onset are distinct objects; their ratio closes and reverses near gamma ~= 0.69.
flagship.primaryMetric: A_PD(gamma) / A_c(gamma), with A_PD located by Floquet multiplier rho=-1 and A_c from the closed-form Melnikov integral.
trustContract: A_c is analytic and pinned by quadrature in tests.
A_PD is measured on the attractor branch and refined by the monodromy/Floquet multiplier crossing rho=-1.
The gamma=0.5 onset is anchored to the Baker-Gollub literature value.
0-1 test samples corroborate regular/chaotic sides without replacing the Floquet criterion.
Every artifact carries parameters, commands, hashes, and caveats.
caveats: The comparison fixes omega=2/3 and follows the primary attractor branch; coexisting basins may have different events.
Melnikov theory is first-order in forcing/damping and is not an ordering bound at strong damping.
Chaotic comparisons are bounded by the predictability horizon.
